title: Actinocephalus incanus
taxonomy: Actinocephalus incanus is classified within the family Eriocaulaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pipeworts. This family is part of the larger order Poales, which includes various grasses and other monocots. The order Poales itself is a part of the larger class Liliopsida, also known as monocots. Monocots are characterized by having one cotyledon or seed leaf in their seeds. Actinocephalus incanus is a unique species within its classification due to its specific characteristics and growth habits.
